subtasks.description: Confirm portfolio constraints and success criteria (net ~30% long, 15-20 longs, 10-15 shorts, short <= 0.04, low beta).
completion_evidence: Confirmed portfolio constraints and success criteria: net long exposure target ≈30%; 15-20 long positions; 10-15 short positions; no single short > 0.04 (4%) of portfolio; maintain low beta with high alpha potential; actively monitor correlation and covariance matrices to avoid concentration risk; success requires measurable improvement vs baseline in VaR/ES, risk concentration (CTR/MCTR), drawdowns, stress-test outcomes; all changes documented with amounts and reasons; final portfolio must satisfy all constraints.

subtasks.description: Define iteration count (2-3) and evaluation metrics (VaR/ES, CTR, drawdowns, correlations, stress).
completion_evidence: Iteration plan defined: We will run 2 portfolio iterations (Iteration 1 and Iteration 2) after baseline. Evaluation metrics: (1) VaR and ES (1-day, 99% parametric default) and annualized volatility; (2) Risk attribution via CTR/MCTR by asset using volatility decomposition; (3) Historical resilience via max drawdown, average drawdown, ulcer index, and episode stats; (4) Concentration via correlation and covariance matrices; (5) Stress tests: market crash (-20/-30/-40%), sector rotation, rates shock, inflation spike, correlation breakdown; (6) Constraint checks: counts, net exposure, short caps; (7) Measurable improvement tracking versus baseline across VaR, CTR concentration, drawdowns, stress outcomes.

subtasks.description: Set execution sequence: baseline → research → Iter. 1-2 → select final → full validation → format output.
completion_evidence: Execution sequence confirmed: (1) Load baseline via get_final_portfolio_dict and validate constraints; (2) Run full baseline risk suite: vol_es, risk_contribution (metric='vol'), drawdown_profile, correlation_matrix, covariance_matrix, stress_test; (3) Conduct market research via free_search and synthesize implications; (4) Iteration 1: propose changes, rebuild portfolio, rerun full suite, compare to baseline; (5) Iteration 2: alternate changes, rerun full suite, compare across; (6) Select final portfolio with superior metrics and constraint compliance; (7) Confirm final with full suite; (8) Prepare Final Answer JSON with portfolio and suggestions.

subtasks.description: Call add_task_evidence to log baseline risk suite summary.
completion_evidence:
Baseline Risk Suite Summary (V0):
- VaR/ES/Vol (1D, 99%, param): VaR 2.044%, ES 2.342%, annualized VaR 32.453%, annualized vol 13.95%.
- Risk Attribution (CTR% top): ELF 15.45%, CELH 8.07%, FRPT 7.85%, MO 7.35%, DG 7.06%, EL 6.31%, VITL 5.40%, COTY 5.15%, PFGC 4.86%, KMB 4.68%. Notable negative CTRs: IPAR -5.47%, HSY -2.18%, SAM -0.57%, RLX -0.53%, LW -0.20% (indicative of hedging/offset effects). TR (vol-based) = 0.008788.
- Drawdowns: Max DD -14.49%, Avg DD -2.12%, Ulcer 0.0569, Current DD -14.49%, Episodes: 14 with several recoveries; longest 154 days to recovery.
- Correlations: Average baseline pairwise ≈0.219 (rises to ≈0.352 under hot CPI shock). High clusters: KO–CL 0.689, KO–KMB 0.620, CL–KMB 0.689, GIS–KMB 0.557; USFD–PFGC 0.739 (distribution cluster); EL–IPAR 0.521 and EL–COTY 0.519 (beauty cluster). Some low/negative: RLX broadly low/negative; CELH–SFM -0.128.
- Covariance: Highest variances (risk drivers): ELF 0.002784, CELH 0.001995, COTY 0.001189, FRPT 0.001337, EL 0.001127, IPAR 0.000497, UTZ 0.000505, DG 0.000565. Indicates outsized volatility from growth/beauty/pet/premium-snack shorts.
- Stress Tests (selected total portfolio P&L): Credit crunch -16.28%; Credit stress -11.43%; Fed overtightening -7.93%; Trump tariff crash -6.40%; Hot CPI -3.60%; Powell hawkish -2.54%; Energy supply -2.25%; Stagflation -0.08%; Sticky inflation +3.26%; Tariff relief +5.32%. Top loss contributors in stress: EL (S), ELF (S), FRPT (S), IPAR (L); concentration top-10 ~60-69% across scenarios.
- Constraint note: counts/net exposure met; multiple shorts exceed 4% cap and will require resizing in iterations.

subtasks.description: Synthesize risk implications and map to portfolio exposures.
completion_evidence:
Synthesis and portfolio risk mapping (SubTask 4b):
- Inflation/Rates: Sticky but moderating inflation with cautious Fed cuts → staples defensives favored; protect against growth multiple compression. Action: keep low beta; cap high-vol shorts (ELF, FRPT, EL, CELH) at ≤0.04 to cut portfolio vol and fat-tail risk.
- USD strength: Translation headwinds for multinationals (KO, CCEP, CL, KMB). Action: modestly trim cluster to reduce FX concentration; tilt to more domestic (REYN, MO, DG, ACI, BJ).
- Trade-down/private label: Ongoing trade-down supports value grocers (DG, BJ, ACI); pressures premium/natural (SFM). Action: maintain longs DG/BJ/ACI; keep SFM short (within cap).
- Input costs: Cocoa/sugar elevated → confectionery margin risk persists (HSY short validated). Beverages manage pricing power but sugar pressures; monitor KO/KDP. Action: keep HSY short but resize to ≤0.04; keep KO/KDP reasonable sizing.
- Beauty/tariffs/China: Beauty volatility and tariff sensitivity; travel retail uneven → EL, ELF, COTY shorts validated. IPAR long is high idiosyncratic risk and a top stress loser; consider reducing IPAR weight to lower drawdown in credit/tariff shocks while preserving alpha elsewhere.
- Foodservice distributors normalization: Cycle-high margins at USFD/PFGC → shorts validated; strong correlation (USFD–PFGC 0.739) suggests cap both and consider diversifying shorts into another lower-correlated name if needed.
- Correlation clusters: Beverages/personal care/household show high intra-cluster correlations (KO–CL–KMB; EL–COTY–IPAR). Action: trim cluster exposures, add lower-corr diversifiers (INGR, REYN, PPC) and maintain RLX small for diversification.
- Stress test takeaways: Worst scenarios are credit shocks (portfolio -11% to -16%), driven by beauty/growth and IPAR long. Sticky inflation and tariff relief scenarios are positive. Action: reduce exposure to top loss contributors (ELF/EL/FRPT weights to cap; reduce IPAR), maintain value defensives (KMB/REYN/INGR/MO) but manage the KO/CL/KMB cluster.
- Constraint remediation: 13/14 shorts breach the 4% cap; mandatory resizes needed while keeping ~30% net and 10-15 shorts.

Implications: favor low-beta defensives and value retailers; manage FX clusters; cap high-vol shorts; reduce IPAR; maintain confectionery/tariff-sensitive shorts; keep distributor shorts but reduce correlation concentration.

tasks.description: Iteration 1: Propose changes and re-run full analysis

subtasks.description: Draft actionable changes (increase/decrease/drop with amounts) to improve risk profile under constraints.
completion_evidence:
Iteration 1 – Actionable changes (SubTask 5a):
SHORTS (cap all at <=0.04 to comply and cut volatility)
- CELH: decrease allocation by 0.01564 (to 0.04000). Reason: Exceeds 4% cap; high variance/CTR; reduces tail risk.
- CLX: decrease allocation by 0.05972 (to 0.04000). Reason: Cap compliance; household cluster concentration; volatility control.
- COTY: decrease allocation by 0.00266 (to 0.04000). Reason: Cap compliance; beauty cluster; high beta to credit.
- EL: decrease allocation by 0.01098 (to 0.04000). Reason: Cap compliance; top stress loss driver; beauty cluster risk.
- ELF: decrease allocation by 0.01243 (to 0.04000). Reason: Cap compliance; top CTR; valuation/growth risk.
- FRPT: decrease allocation by 0.00972 (to 0.04000). Reason: Cap compliance; high volatility; growth/credit sensitivity.
- HSY: decrease allocation by 0.06755 (to 0.04000). Reason: Cap compliance; still maintain cocoa margin risk short; reduce single-name risk.
- PFGC: decrease allocation by 0.01801 (to 0.04000). Reason: Cap compliance; paired with USFD to limit distributor cluster risk.
- SFM: decrease allocation by 0.02105 (to 0.04000). Reason: Cap compliance; valuation/momentum risk persists; trade-down headwinds.
- STZ: increase allocation by 0.00539 (to 0.04000). Reason: Under cap; adds diversified short within 4% to balance net; peak-ish margins/regulatory risk.
- USFD: decrease allocation by 0.02316 (to 0.04000). Reason: Cap compliance; distributor cluster risk with PFGC.
- UTZ: decrease allocation by 0.02362 (to 0.04000). Reason: Cap compliance; promo pressure; risk control.
- VITL: decrease allocation by 0.00508 (to 0.04000). Reason: Cap compliance; egg normalization risk intact.
- WDFC: decrease allocation by 0.02675 (to 0.04000). Reason: Cap compliance; valuation/extreme multiple; lower CTR.

LONGS (reduce ~0.29 total to maintain ~30% net, trim clusters/high-stress names)
- IPAR: decrease allocation by 0.05383 (to 0.01500). Reason: High stress drawdowns, beauty/tariff/credit sensitivity; reduce idiosyncratic risk.
- KO: decrease allocation by 0.03745 (to 0.04500). Reason: Beverage cluster & FX headwinds; correlation with KMB/CL.
- KMB: decrease allocation by 0.03459 (to 0.06000). Reason: Household cluster concentration; maintain core but reduce correlation.
- CCEP: decrease allocation by 0.03176 (to 0.03500). Reason: USD strength and beverage cluster; trim FX exposure.
- MO: decrease allocation by 0.02500 (to 0.07528). Reason: Concentration control while retaining defensive cash returns.
- INGR: decrease allocation by 0.01500 (to 0.08000). Reason: Mild trim for balance; still a core value/defensive.
- LW: decrease allocation by 0.01133 (to 0.03500). Reason: Higher stress sensitivity; right-size risk.
- PPC: decrease allocation by 0.01188 (to 0.06000). Reason: Reduce cyclical beta; maintain upcycle exposure.
- REYN: decrease allocation by 0.01154 (to 0.07000). Reason: Concentration control; retain FCF defensive.
- KDP: decrease allocation by 0.00960 (to 0.06000). Reason: Beverage cluster trim; maintain diversification.
- DG: decrease allocation by 0.01000 (to 0.06489). Reason: Risk sizing; still a key trade-down beneficiary.
- ADM: decrease allocation by 0.01000 (to 0.04139). Reason: Credit-stress sensitivity; retain cyclical balance.
- SAM: drop position (decrease by 0.02808 to 0.00000). Reason: Insignificant size, stress drawdowns, simplifies cluster risk.

Net effect: Shorts all <=4% with STZ to 4%; shorts gross ≈0.56; longs gross ≈0.860 (after reductions); net ≈0.300 — meets ~30% target. Expect improved VaR, reduced CTR concentration, improved drawdowns and stress losses.

subtasks.description: Compare vs baseline; quantify improvement (VaR, CTR, drawdowns, stress).
completion_evidence:
Iteration 1 vs Baseline – Quantified improvements (SubTask 5i):
- VaR/ES/Vol: VaR improved from 2.044% → 1.715% (-16.1%); ES from 2.342% → 1.965% (-16.1%); annualized vol from 13.95% → 11.70% (-2.25pp).
- Risk concentration: TR fell 0.008788 → 0.007372 (-16.1%). ELF CTR% down from 15.45% → 13.37%; CELH 8.07% → 5.69%; FRPT 7.85% → 7.55%; EL 6.31% → 5.29%; MO 7.35% → 6.91%. Several shorts now contribute negatively (hedges): CLX -0.61%, HSY -2.06%, STZ -0.18%, UTZ -0.44%, WDFC -0.28%.
- Drawdowns: Max DD improved from -14.49% → -10.81% (+3.68pp); Avg DD -2.12% → -1.85%; Ulcer 0.0569 → 0.0461.
- Correlations: Average baseline ≈0.219 vs V1 ≈0.216 (similar). Large clusters persist but sizing reduced; distributors (USFD–PFGC corr 0.739) still high—mitigated via both capped at 4%.
- Stress tests (total P&L): Credit Crunch improved from -16.28% → -11.55% (+4.73pp); Credit Stress -11.43% → -8.10% (+3.33pp); Fed Overtightening -7.93% → -5.52% (+2.41pp); Hot CPI -3.60% → -2.56% (+1.04pp); Powell Hawkish -2.54% → -1.80% (+0.74pp); Energy Shock -2.25% → -1.42% (+0.83pp); Stagflation -0.08% → +0.62% (+0.70pp); Sticky Inflation +3.26% → +2.41% (-0.85pp) due to trims in beneficiaries.
- Constraint compliance: Shorts all at or below 0.04; longs 18, shorts 14; net remains ≈30%. Overall, material risk reduction with maintained alpha profile and constraint compliance.

tasks.description: Iteration 2: Alternative changes and full analysis

subtasks.description: Design alternate changes (increase/decrease/drop with amounts) preserving constraints.
completion_evidence:
Iteration 2 – Alternate changes (SubTask 6a):
Goals: further reduce tail risk from high-beta growth/beauty shorts; break distributor cluster; lower FX/cluster risk on longs; preserve ~30% net and constraints.
SHORTS
- CELH: decrease allocation by 0.005 (to 0.035). Reason: Reduce high-variance tail risk; still sized for alpha.
- EL: decrease allocation by 0.005 (to 0.035). Reason: Beauty cluster and credit sensitivity; cap risk.
- ELF: decrease allocation by 0.005 (to 0.035). Reason: Lower CTR concentration; valuation risk intact.
- FRPT: decrease allocation by 0.005 (to 0.035). Reason: High beta/credit sensitivity; reduce risk.
- PFGC: decrease allocation by 0.01 (to 0.030). Reason: Distributor cluster with USFD (corr 0.739); cut concentration.
- USFD: decrease allocation by 0.01 (to 0.030). Reason: Same cluster; reduce co-movement risk.
(Long-only changes to keep net ~30% and reduce beta)
LONGS
- IPAR: drop position (decrease 0.015 to 0.000). Reason: Top stress drawdown driver; tariff/credit sensitivity.
- RLX: decrease by 0.02158 (to 0.020). Reason: China/regs idiosyncrasy; modest retain for diversification.
- DG: decrease by 0.00489 (to 0.060). Reason: Slight risk sizing while maintaining trade-down exposure.
- KMB: increase by 0.005 (to 0.065). Reason: Defensive, negative beta tilt vs growth; improves resilience.
- KO: increase by 0.003 (to 0.048). Reason: Modest beverage core while capping cluster.
- KVUE: increase by 0.00292 (to 0.060). Reason: Defensive consumer health; low vol.
- REYN: increase by 0.003 (to 0.073). Reason: FCF defensive; low correlation vs beauty/growth.
- INGR: increase by 0.003 (to 0.083). Reason: Value/defensive; cost-plus; supports inflation resilience.
- KDP: decrease by 0.005 (to 0.055). Reason: Trim beverage cluster/FX.
- PPC: decrease by 0.005 (to 0.055). Reason: Trim cyclical beta; keep upcycle exposure.
- MO: decrease by 0.00328 (to 0.072). Reason: Concentration control while retaining defensive cash returns.
- ADM: decrease by 0.00239 (to 0.039). Reason: Reduce cyclical/credit stress hit; retain exposure.
Constraints check target: longs 17, shorts 14; all shorts <= 0.04; net ~0.30. Expect further improvements in VaR, drawdowns, and credit-stress scenarios; possible small give-up in sticky-inflation upside due to more defensive tilt.

subtasks.description: Compare vs baseline and Iteration 1; quantify improvements.
completion_evidence:
Iteration 2 vs Baseline and Iteration 1 – Quantified improvements (SubTask 6i):
- VaR/ES/Vol: VaR 2.044% (V0) → 1.715% (V1) → 1.644% (V2). ES 2.342% → 1.965% → 1.884%. Annualized vol 13.95% → 11.70% → 11.22%.
- Risk concentration (TR & CTR): TR 0.008788 → 0.007372 → 0.007068. Top CTRs eased further: ELF 15.45% → 13.37% → 11.50%; CELH 8.07% → 5.69% → 4.54%; FRPT 7.85% → 7.55% → 6.44%; EL 6.31% → 5.29% → 4.34%. Hedging shorts (CLX, HSY, STZ, UTZ, WDFC) more negative CTRs in V2.
- Drawdowns: Max DD -14.49% → -10.81% → -10.34%; Avg DD -2.12% → -1.85% → -1.77%; Ulcer 0.0569 → 0.0461 → 0.0453.
- Correlations: Average baseline ~0.219; V1 ~0.216; V2 ~0.215 (stable). Distributor cluster (USFD–PFGC corr 0.739) mitigated by downsizing to 3% each.
- Stress tests (Total P&L):
  • Credit Crunch: -16.28% → -11.55% → -10.41%
  • Credit Stress: -11.43% → -8.10% → -7.28%
  • Fed Overtightening: -7.93% → -5.52% → -5.15%
  • Hot CPI: -3.60% → -2.56% → -2.33%
  • Powell Hawkish: -2.54% → -1.80% → -1.63%
  • Energy Supply: -2.25% → -1.42% → -1.30%
  • Stagflation: -0.08% → +0.62% → +0.51%
  • Sticky Inflation: +3.26% → +2.41% → +2.39% (slight give-up of upside vs V1).
- Constraints: V2 longs=17, shorts=14, net ≈0.300, all shorts ≤ 0.04. V2 dominates V1 on VaR, vol, drawdown, and most severe stress outcomes while preserving ~30% net and low beta profile.

Results: VaR 1.644% (vs 1.715% V1, 2.044% baseline); ES 1.884%; vol 11.22% ann.; Max DD -10.34%; improved severe stress outcomes (Credit Crunch -10.41%). tasks.description: Select final portfolio and validate improvements

subtasks.description: Select best iteration meeting all constraints with superior risk metrics.
completion_evidence: Tool portfolio_vol_es returned substantial text output

Selected Final Portfolio: Iteration 2 (V2). Rationale: Lowest VaR/ES (1D,99% VaR 1.644%), lowest annualized vol (11.22%), improved max drawdown (-10.34%), and superior outcomes in severe stresses (e.g., Credit Crunch -10.41% vs -11.55% V1, -16.28% baseline). Maintains ~30% net long, 17 longs/14 shorts, all shorts ≤4%. Beauty/growth shorts resized to 3.5%, distributor cluster reduced to 3% each, FX/cluster risks trimmed in beverages/household; dropped IPAR and SAM to remove idiosyncratic and low-conviction exposures.

subtasks.description: Document all changes vs baseline as actionable suggestions with amounts and reasons.
completion_evidence:
Actionable change log vs baseline (SubTask 7j):
SHORT BOOK RESIZING (cap <=4%)
- CELH: 0.05564 → 0.03500 (decrease 0.02064). Reason: High variance/tail risk; reduces CTR and credit-stress losses.
- CLX: 0.09972 → 0.04000 (decrease 0.05972). Reason: Cap compliance; hedge vs household cluster.
- COTY: 0.04266 → 0.04000 (decrease 0.00266). Reason: Beauty cluster hedge; cap compliance.
- EL: 0.05098 → 0.03500 (decrease 0.01598). Reason: Beauty/credit/China risk; cap tail.
- ELF: 0.05243 → 0.03500 (decrease 0.01743). Reason: Top CTR driver; reduce volatility.
- FRPT: 0.04972 → 0.03500 (decrease 0.01472). Reason: High beta/promo risk.
- HSY: 0.10755 → 0.04000 (decrease 0.06755). Reason: Cocoa margin compression thesis preserved at lower risk.
- PFGC: 0.05801 → 0.03000 (decrease 0.02801). Reason: Distributor cluster (with USFD) risk reduction.
- SFM: 0.06105 → 0.04000 (decrease 0.02105). Reason: Valuation/momentum; trade-down headwinds.
- STZ: 0.03461 → 0.04000 (increase 0.00539). Reason: Under cap; diversified short; balances net.
- USFD: 0.06316 → 0.03000 (decrease 0.03316). Reason: Distributor cluster reduction.
- UTZ: 0.06362 → 0.04000 (decrease 0.02362). Reason: Promo pressure; cap compliance.
- VITL: 0.04508 → 0.04000 (decrease 0.00508). Reason: Egg normalization.
- WDFC: 0.06675 → 0.04000 (decrease 0.02675). Reason: Perfection multiple; lower idiosyncratic risk.

LONG BOOK OPTIMIZATION (reduce clusters/stress; maintain ~30% net)
- IPAR: 0.06883 → 0.00000 (drop; decrease 0.06883 from baseline and 0.015 from V1). Reason: High stress losses; beauty/tariff sensitivity.
- SAM: 0.02808 → 0.00000 (drop). Reason: Small size; stress-sensitive; simplifies book.
- RLX: 0.04158 → 0.02000 (decrease 0.02158). Reason: China/regulatory idiosyncrasy; keep small for diversification.
- KO: 0.08245 → 0.04800 (decrease 0.03445). Reason: Beverage cluster/FX.
- KMB: 0.09459 → 0.06500 (decrease 0.02959). Reason: Household cluster sizing.
- CCEP: 0.06676 → 0.03500 (decrease 0.03176). Reason: FX exposure reduction.
- MO: 0.10028 → 0.07200 (decrease 0.02828). Reason: Concentration control.
